serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Rojak Bandung is approximately 180 grams, equivalent to one standard plate as commonly served at Malaysian hawker stalls.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritional Composition of Malaysian Foods (IMR Malaysia MyFCD) — closest analogous entry for mixed vegetable and seafood salad with shrimp paste dressing
nutrient_source.organization: Institute for Medical Research (IMR) Malaysia — MyFCD
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Rojak Bandung in IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Nutrient values are adapted from the input enriched JSON (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.9), cross-referenced against IMR MyFCD component entries for blanched water spinach, cuttlefish, cucumber, shrimp paste, peanuts, and sesame seeds. Values are plausible for this dish composition. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy used as secondary cross-reference for individual ingredient macros.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — analogous estimate for mixed vegetable salad with sweet sauce dressing
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for Rojak Bandung. GI of 50 is estimated by analogy to mixed vegetable salads with sweet sauce dressings (GI range 45–55) from the University of Sydney GI Database. The moderate sugar content from the shrimp paste dressing is partially offset by dietary fibre from water spinach and cucumber. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (50 × 14.5 × 1.8) / 100 = 13.05, rounded to 13.1.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from shrimp paste (petis udang)
hypertension.guidance: Rojak Bandung contains approximately 380 mg sodium per 100g (684 mg per standard 180g serving), primarily from shrimp paste; individuals with hypertension should strictly limit or avoid this dish and request reduced-paste preparation where possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar in shrimp paste dressing contributing 9g sugar per 100g
type2_diabetes.guidance: The sweet shrimp paste dressing contributes significant added sugar (approximately 16.2g per 180g serving); individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size, request reduced-sugar dressing, and monitor postprandial glucose response given the estimated GI of 50 and glycemic load of approximately 13 per serving.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate fat from peanuts and sesame seeds
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high sodium content (380 mg/100g) poses a cardiovascular risk for individuals with existing heart disease; however, the unsaturated fat profile from peanuts and sesame seeds is relatively favorable, and the dish provides beneficial micronutrients including potassium (210 mg/100g) and vitamin K (110 mcg/100g) from water spinach.

shellfish_allergy.guidance: Rojak Bandung contains both cuttlefish (sotong) and shrimp paste (petis udang); individuals with shellfish or crustacean allergies must avoid this dish entirely.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Rojak Bandung is a traditional Malaysian savory salad originating from the Malay culinary tradition. It is composed of blanched water spinach (kangkung), blanched or boiled cuttlefish (sotong), and fresh cucumber, all tossed in a thick, dark, and intensely flavored shrimp paste sauce (petis udang) that is seasoned with chili, sugar, and lime juice. The dish is finished with crushed peanuts and toasted sesame seeds, which add crunch and nuttiness to the otherwise soft and chewy textures of the main ingredients. The flavor profile is a complex interplay of umami from the shrimp paste, sweetness from added sugar, acidity from lime, and heat from chili. Nutritionally, Rojak Bandung provides approximately 130 kcal per 100g, with 6.5g protein largely from cuttlefish, 5.2g fat from peanuts and sesame, and 14.5g carbohydrate including 9g sugar from the dressing. It is a notable source of vitamin A (320 mcg RAE/100g) and vitamin K (110 mcg/100g) from water spinach, and provides vitamin B12 (1.2 mcg/100g) from cuttlefish. The high sodium content (380 mg/100g) from shrimp paste makes it unsuitable for individuals on low-sodium diets or those managing hypertension. It is commonly served as a side dish or street food snack at Malaysian hawker stalls during lunch, dinner, or as a snack.

ingredient_synonyms.kangkung: water spinach, water morning glory, swamp cabbage, Ipomoea aquatica
ingredient_synonyms.petis udang: shrimp paste, prawn paste, dark shrimp paste, Malaysian black shrimp paste
ingredient_synonyms.rojak: Malaysian mixed salad, savory fruit and vegetable salad with paste dressing

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Rojak Bandung was found in IMR Malaysia MyFCD or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from the input enriched JSON (ai_generated, confidence 0.9) and cross-referenced against approved source component entries for the constituent ingredients. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the absence of a direct approved-source match for the composite dish.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from the input enriched JSON (provenance: ai_generated) and cross-referenced against IMR Malaysia MyFCD component entries and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for individual ingredients (water spinach, cuttlefish, cucumber, shrimp paste, peanuts, sesame seeds). No exact composite dish entry was found in any approved primary source. 2. cholesterol_mg set to -1: detailed cholesterol data for this specific Rojak Bandung preparation is not available in IMR Malaysia MyFCD or USDA FoodData Central as a composite dish entry. 3. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this composite dish preparation is not reported in available approved sources. 4. saturated_fat_g (0.9), monounsaturated_fat_g (2.1), and polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.8) are estimated from component ingredient fatty acid profiles using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for peanuts, sesame seeds, and cuttlefish; these are approximate values. 5. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 as this dish contains no hydrogenated fats by preparation method. 6. GI of 50 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Rojak Bandung was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 7. Vitamin A value of 320 mcg RAE/100g reflects the high contribution of water spinach (kangkung), which is a rich source of beta-carotene. 8. The input source cited (University of Malaya, Faculty of Medicine) is not on the approved source list; values have been re-anchored to IMR Malaysia MyFCD and USDA FoodData Central as the approved primary sources.
